metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Which country is the host of World Seafood Congress (WSC) 2026?
questions.explanation: India will host the World Seafood Congress (WSC) 2026 in Chennai, marking the first time the biennial event is held in India. The Congress focuses on seafood trade, safety, innovation, and sustainability. India is the sixth-largest global seafood exporter and second-largest aquaculture producer, exporting 1.78 million tonnes valued at ₹60,523.89 crore (USD 7.38 billion) in 2023–24. The Congress will feature a trade fair, plenary sessions, panel discussions, technical presentations, and workshops

questions.question: World Animal Welfare Day is celebrated annually on which day?
questions.explanation: World Animal Welfare Day is observed every year on October 4. The day aims to raise global awareness about animal well-being. It promotes humane treatment of animals across the world. The theme for World Animal Welfare Day 2025 is “Save Animals, Save the Planet!” 2025 marks the 100th anniversary of World Animal Day. The theme emphasizes that protecting animal lives supports a healthier and sustainable planet. The first World Animal Day was organized in 1925 by Heinrich Zimmermann, a German novelist and animal welfare campaigner.

questions.question: What is the theme of Manipur Global Buyers and Sellers Summit 2025?
questions.explanation: The Manipur Global Buyers and Sellers Summit 2025 began at the City Convention Centre, Imphal. The two-day summit is held under the theme “Bridging Business Sans Borders”. It is part of the Raising and Accelerating MSME Performance (RAMP) initiative by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India. The summit is jointly organised by the Directorate of Trade, Commerce and Industries, Government of Manipur, Manipur Industrial Development Corporation Ltd, and Manipur Chamber of Commerce and Industry. The summit highlights Manipur’s natural abundance, creativity, and resilience, featuring products like black rice, organic produce, handloom, and handicrafts.

questions.question: Which department of India hosted the ‘AI for Good Summit’ at India Mobile Congress 2025?

options.label: B
options.text: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances

options.label: C
options.text: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade

questions.explanation: Recently, the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) and International Telecommunication Union (ITU) hosted the ‘AI for Good Summit’ at India Mobile Congress (IMC) 2025 in New Delhi. ITU is the United Nations (UN) specialized agency for information and communication technologies (ICT). It was established in 1865 as the International Telegraph Union. In 1947, ITU became a specialized agency of the UN. ITU coordinates between governments and private sector bodies for global telecommunication and ICT services.

questions.question: Which month is marked as Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS) Awareness Month?
questions.explanation: October is observed as Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS) Month to raise awareness. Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS) is the sudden and unexpected death of an infant under one year of age with no identifiable cause after investigation. It is also called ‘cot death’ or ‘crib death,’ though cots and cribs are not the cause. Most SIDS cases occur within the first six months of life. Boys are slightly more affected, and deaths usually happen during sleep. Risk factors include premature birth, low birth weight, family history of SIDS, exposure to smoking, unsafe sleeping conditions, and poor prenatal care.

questions.question: International Day for the Eradication of Poverty is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: International Day for the Eradication of Poverty 2025 is observed on October 17. The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) renews its commitment to eradicate poverty and promote shared prosperity. This year’s theme is “Ending social and institutional maltreatment by ensuring respect and effective support for families.” The 2025 Global Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI), by UNDP and Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative, shows 887 million poor people—nearly 80%—face at least one major climate hazard.

questions.question: The International Day of the Intangible Cultural Heritage (IDICH) is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: The International Day of the Intangible Cultural Heritage (IDICH) is observed annually on October 17 to promote awareness of preserving the world’s living heritage. In November 2023, the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) adopted resolution A/RES/42/34, declaring October 17 as Intangible Cultural Heritage (IDICH). The day commemorates the 2003 Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage, effective from 2006.

questions.question: Which day is observed as Police Commemoration Day every year in India?
questions.explanation: Police Commemoration Day is observed annually on October 21 to honour police personnel martyred at Hot Springs, Ladakh, in 1959 and all who sacrificed their lives in the line of duty. Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid tribute to police personnel for their courage, dedication, and supreme sacrifices, highlighting their role in keeping the nation safe. Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) and Central Police Organisations (CPOs) organise events from October 22–30, including family visits, police band displays, motorcycle rallies, “Run for Fallen Policemen,” blood donation camps, and competitions for children.

questions.question: World Iodine Deficiency Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Iodine Deficiency Day is observed every year on 21st October to raise awareness about the importance of iodine for good health. It is also known as World Iodine Deficiency Disorder Day. The day highlights the need to prevent iodine deficiency disorders (IDDs), which can cause goitre, mental retardation, and other health issues. Major organisations like the World Health Organisation (WHO) and UNICEF organise campaigns, workshops, and educational programs globally. The initiative began after the 1990 World Summit for Children, where iodine’s significance was recognised. By 2002, global awareness programs expanded, leading to the formal observance of World Iodine Deficiency Day.

questions.question: Where was the United Nations (UN) Convention against Cybercrime held in October 2025?
questions.explanation: In October 2025, the United Nations (UN) Convention against Cybercrime was held in Hanoi, Vietnam, marking a major global step against cybercrime. The signing ceremony was co-hosted by Vietnam and the UN Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC). The Convention opened for signature on 25–26 October 2025 in Hanoi and remains open at the UN Headquarters, New York (USA) till 31 December 2026. The aim is to create the world’s first global legal framework to prevent, investigate, and prosecute cybercrimes. It covers ransomware, financial fraud, and non-consensual sharing of intimate images. It is the first treaty to criminalise the non-consensual sharing of intimate images, strengthening digital rights protection.
